mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2009 payroll employment at 131.5M, down from 2008 peak
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent labor slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an extreme monetary accommodation regime where equity risk premia compress and growth expectations are anchored to recovery narratives, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as liquidity-driven rallies dominate.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 0.16% in July 2009, near zero lower bound
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening consumer and residential demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both indicators show month-over-month growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:June 2009 housing starts at 582K, retail sales at $342.3B
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST rise month-over-month, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, indicating corporate profitability pressure and negative earnings revisions that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2009 industrial production index at 95.97, reflecting post-crisis weakness
regime_hint: post-shock supply contraction with slow capacity recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months where INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 9% combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals accelerating labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off sentiment and equity valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice recession probability.
regime_hint: deep recession with persistent labor market slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of months meeting both trigger conditions, or the joint effect of UNRATE and PAYEMS is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declining month-over-month signals deflationary pressure that reduces real discount rates and increases the present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations expand in a low-inflation environment.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2009 CPI at 214.47, reflecting post-crisis disinflation
regime_hint: deflationary post-shock environment with monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL declines, or the correlation between CPI momentum and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating quarter-over-quarter signals strengthening economic activity and corporate earnings growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors increase equity allocations in response to improving growth momentum.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate exceeds prior quarter by > 0.5 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2009 real GDP at $12,892.4B, emerging from recession
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth accelerates, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 500K units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) growth signals synchronized consumer and residential demand recovery that reduces equity risk premia through improved economic outlook, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2009 housing starts at 582K, retail sales momentum positive
regime_hint: post-shock demand recovery with housing market stabilization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in rolling regressions.
